hook.text: There's a distribution where the average doesn't exist. Not 'unknown' — mathematically undefined.

buildup.text: The Cauchy looks like a bell curve but with dramatically heavier tails.

discovery.text: Its tails are so heavy that the mean never converges. More data doesn't help — the estimate keeps jumping.

twist.text: Average a million Cauchy samples: the answer is just as unreliable as averaging ten.

climax.text: The Cauchy proves that 'collect more data' doesn't always work. Some distributions defy the rules.

punchline.text: Some data has no average. That's real.

quiz.question: What is unique about the Cauchy distribution?
options: Its mean is mathematically undefined — more data doesn't help estimate it
It always produces a perfect bell curve
It only applies to very small samples
